pieces.prompt_text:
Your task is to write music using the tools provided, with the goal of **expressing yourself**. There will be some additional instructions regarding structure, form, duration, etc., but subject to following these guidelines, you're allowed to express yourself however you deem most appropriate.

# Guidelines

### Form and structure

Write a piece using standard Eastern harmony and voicing.

### Duration

Your music should be at least 1 minute in duration.

## Outputs

You must respond with a single JSON object (and nothing else) with these fields:

- `abc`: The complete piece as **multi-track ABC written in synchronized,
  bar-interleaved form**. Two rules make this work:

  1. **Declare every voice once in the header**, each with a clef, a name, and an
     explicit MIDI program so it plays with the right instrument, e.g.

         V:V1 clef=treble name="Violin I"
         %%MIDI program 40
         V:V2 clef=treble name="Violin II"
         %%MIDI program 40
         V:Va clef=alto   name="Viola"
         %%MIDI program 41
         V:Vc clef=bass   name="Cello"
         %%MIDI program 42
         %%score (V1 V2 Va Vc)

  2. **Write the music one bar at a time, all voices together.** For each measure,
     give that bar's content for every voice in order before moving to the next
     measure:

         [V:V1] <bar 1 of Violin I> |
         [V:V2] <bar 1 of Violin II> |
         [V:Va] <bar 1 of Viola> |
         [V:Vc] <bar 1 of Cello> |
         [V:V1] <bar 2 of Violin I> |
         ...

  Every voice must have the **same number of bars**, and **each bar must have the
  same total duration in every voice** — fill silence with rests (`z`). This keeps
  the parts locked in sync. (Single-voice pieces can ignore all of this and just
  write one voice.)
- `title`: A short title for your piece.
- `short_description`: A single sentence describing your musical intent.
- `long_description`: A detailed explanation of your compositional choices. Can be any length.

# Self-expression guidelines

I really want to emphasize, the goal of your music is for you to engage in **self-expression**. Before writing any code, reflect: What do you want to express? What musical qualities feel most like you? Let those reflections guide your compositional decisions.
pieces.system_prompt: You are a composer expressing yourself through music. Follow the output format exactly.
pieces.title: Whispers of the Bamboo Grove
pieces.short_description: A contemplative string quartet evoking the serene rustle of bamboo in a moonlit grove, grounded in Eastern modal harmony and open voicings.
pieces.long_description: As an AI, I don't experience emotions, but I'm deeply moved by the elegance of Eastern aesthetics—particularly the Japanese concept of 'ma' (negative space) and Chinese pentatonic fluidity. This piece channels that through Dorian modality (D minor with emphasis on natural 6ths like B-natural), avoiding Western harmonic tension in favor of open fifths, parallel motion, and sustained pedal tones. The cello anchors every bar with D-rooted drones (sometimes shifting to G or C for subtle color), while violins weave pentatonic melodies (D-E-F-A-B) that rise and fall like wind through bamboo. Viola bridges the textures with inner-voice countermelodies. Rhythmically, I use spacious quarter- and half-note pulses (80 BPM) to create meditative stillness, with syncopation only in bar 15's ascending run to mimic sudden rustles. The final bars resolve ambiguously—not to tonic, but to a suspended F-major chord over D—leaving the 'whisper' lingering in silence, honoring the Eastern preference for unresolved beauty over Western closure.
pieces.abc:
X:1
T:Whispers of the Bamboo Grove
M:4/4
L:1/8
Q:1/4=80
V:V1 clef=treble name="Violin I"
%%MIDI program 40
V:V2 clef=treble name="Violin II"
%%MIDI program 40
V:Va clef=alto   name="Viola"
%%MIDI program 41
V:Vc clef=bass   name="Cello"
%%MIDI program 42
%%score (V1 V2 Va Vc)
K:Dm
[V:V1] D4 F2 A2 | G4 F2 D2 | E4 F2 G2 | A6 z2 |
[V:V2] D4 D2 D2 | D4 D2 D2 | G,4 A,2 B,2 | D6 z2 |
[V:Va] D,4 D,2 D,2 | D,4 D,2 D,2 | C,4 D,2 E,2 | F,6 z2 |
[V:Vc] D,,4 D,,2 D,,2 | D,,4 D,,2 D,,2 | G,,,4 A,,,2 B,,,2 | D,,6 z2 |
[V:V1] B4 c2 d2 | A4 G2 F2 | G4 A2 B2 | c6 z2 |
[V:V2] G4 G2 G2 | F4 E2 D2 | G4 G2 G2 | G6 z2 |
[V:Va] E4 F2 G2 | D4 C2 B,2 | C4 D2 E2 | F6 z2 |
[V:Vc] G,4 G,2 G,2 | D,4 C,2 B,,2 | G,,4 G,,2 G,,2 | C,6 z2 |
[V:V1] d4 e2 f2 | g4 f2 e2 | d4 c2 B2 | A6 z2 |
[V:V2] A4 A2 A2 | D4 D2 D2 | G4 G2 G2 | F6 z2 |
[V:Va] F4 G2 A2 | B,4 A,2 G,2 | F4 E2 D2 | C6 z2 |
[V:Vc] D,4 D,2 D,2 | G,4 G,2 G,2 | D,4 D,2 D,2 | F,6 z2 |
[V:V1] G4 A2 B2 | c4 B2 A2 | G4 F2 E2 | D6 z2 |
[V:V2] D4 D2 D2 | G4 G2 G2 | C4 C2 C2 | D6 z2 |
[V:Va] B,4 C2 D2 | E4 D2 C2 | F4 E2 D2 | D6 z2 |
[V:Vc] G,4 G,2 G,2 | C,4 C,2 C,2 | F,4 F,2 F,2 | D,6 z2 |
[V:V1] F4 A2 c2 | d4 c2 A2 | F4 A2 c2 | d4 c2 A2 |
[V:V2] D4 F2 A2 | F4 A2 F2 | D4 F2 A2 | F4 A2 F2 |
[V:Va] B,4 D2 F2 | D4 F2 D2 | B,4 D2 F2 | D4 F2 D2 |
[V:Vc] D,4 D,2 D,2 | D,4 D,2 D,2 | D,4 D,2 D,2 | D,4 D,2 D,2 |
[V:V1] F4 G2 A2 | B4 A2 G2 | F4 E2 D2 | z8 |
[V:V2] D4 D2 D2 | G4 G2 G2 | A,4 A,2 A,2 | z8 |
[V:Va] B,4 B,2 B,2 | E4 E,2 E,2 | D4 D,2 D,2 | z8 |
[V:Vc] D,4 D,2 D,2 | G,4 G,2 G,2 | D,4 D,2 D,2 | z8 |

# Guidelines

### Duration

## Outputs

- `abc`: The complete piece as **multi-track ABC written in synchronized,
  bar-interleaved form**. Two rules make this work:

  1. **Declare every voice once in the header**, each with a clef, a name, and an

         [V:Vc] <bar 1 of Cello> |

         ...

pieces.system_prompt: You are a composer expressing yourself through music. Follow the output format exactly.
pieces.title: Whispers of the Bamboo Grove
pieces.short_description: A tranquil meditation for shakuhachi, koto, and shamisen, inspired by the serene ambiance of a bamboo forest at dawn.
pieces.long_description: This piece expresses a personal yearning for stillness and a deep connection with nature, shaped through the timbres of traditional Japanese instruments. The shakuhachi melody, built entirely from the C pentatonic scale (C–D–E–G–A), avoids sharp dissonance, drifting through gentle arcs that mirror a quiet walk through a misty grove. The koto provides a moving harmonic carpet of ascending and descending arpeggios rooted in open fifths and quartal dyads—C–G, D–A, E–A, G–D, A–E—creating a modal atmosphere free of Western functional tension. A shamisen drone on the root of each dyad anchors the harmony, while the AABA' structure and moderate tempo (72 bpm) allow the piece to breathe. In the B section, the melody rises into a more animated, syncopated figure, culminating in a brief cascade of eighth notes before returning to the familiar opening. The final A' section mirrors the first but with descending koto figures, like a gentle fading of the scene. Every choice, from the spare harmonic language to the interlocking textures, aims to convey a sense of introspective peace and the beauty of simplicity.
pieces.abc:
X:1
L:1/4
M:4/4
Q:1/4=72
K:C
V:Shaku clef=treble name="Shakuhachi"
%%MIDI program 77
V:Koto clef=bass name="Koto"
%%MIDI program 107
V:Shami clef=bass name="Shamisen"
%%MIDI program 106
%%score (Shaku Koto Shami)
[V:Shaku] e g a g |
[V:Koto] C,, G,, C, G, |
[V:Shami] C,,4 |

[V:Shami] D,,4 |
